scale.demand_durability: Medium — DC fuel cells are a real niche, but unproven economics and a net-debt balance sheet that embeds perfection.

BWXT.sector: Industrials / Nuclear (defense + commercial nuclear components, fuel & services, medical isotopes)
BWXT.bull_thesis: BWXT is the closest thing to a sole-source toll on the U.S. nuclear renaissance, and it now sits at the intersection of three converging secular waves: (1) the AI-datacenter power crunch driving SMR/microreactor demand, (2) HALEU/HEU enrichment reshoring (Tennessee NRC engagement), and (3) a defense spine that is effectively un-disruptable. The government franchise — the only U.S. manufacturer of naval nuclear reactors for Virginia/Columbia submarines and Ford carriers — is a regulatory-license + security-clearance monopoly that produced 20.4% segment EBITDA margins and grew 8% in FY25 with multi-decade visibility (long-lead Columbia/Virginia work, the next Ford ship-set). On top of that annuity, commercial nuclear is inflecting hard: +63% FY25 to $853M, +121% in Q1-26, with management raising commercial growth guidance to ~30%. The proof is the backlog: $8.65-8.7B at Q1-26, +77% YoY and +19% sequentially — ~2.3x annual revenue, the strongest visibility in the basket. Management is funding a Made-in-USA commercial-nuclear footprint (PCG acquisition + contemplated Mount Vernon greenfield) precisely as customers demand localized capacity across the U.S., Canada and Europe. Beat-and-raise cadence is intact (Q1-26 EPS $1.12, +22%, beat by ~$0.20); FY26 guidance was lifted across revenue (>$3.75B), EBITDA ($650-665M), EPS ($4.60-4.75) and FCF ($315-330M) after just one quarter. Picks-and-shovels exposure to AI-power and SMR with a defense floor underneath — the rare scale-out name whose demand does NOT collapse if hyperscaler capex pauses, because the Navy keeps paying. Conditions: commercial ramp scales toward government-like margins; greenfield/PCG capex converts to FCF; NRC enrichment milestones advance.
BWXT.bear_thesis: You are paying a wide-moat multiple for a ~10.5%-ROIC compounder, and the AI/SMR/HALEU optionality is already heavily pre-paid. At ~$205, ~$18.7B market cap and ~$19.5B EV, BWXT trades at ~55x trailing / ~44x forward earnings and ~30-33x forward EV/EBITDA — a ~2.4x premium to defense peers — on a business whose ROIC is only ~10.5% and whose FCF yield is ~1.6%. The headline growth flatters the underlying engine: of the 26% Q1-26 revenue growth, organic was only ~11%; the rest is acquired (Kinectrics, A.O.T., now PCG). Commercial nuclear, the re-rating driver, is still a single-digit-to-low-teens share of EBITDA and carries a 12.9% margin that DILUTES the 20.4% government margin as it grows, structurally capping blended-margin expansion. The capital story has quietly degraded from a net-cash buyback compounder (only ~$20M repurchased in 2025, 0.36% yield) to a leverage-up M&A/greenfield spender: ~$1.5B net debt and rising, ~2.3x EBITDA, with Mount Vernon and PCG consuming cash before they generate it. The load-bearing commercial catalysts are years out and regulatory-gated: the Tennessee HEU/HALEU enrichment facility is NRC-process-dependent, SMR component volumes depend on third-party reactor deployments that keep slipping, and medical isotopes remain a slow-burn option. The defense base is real and durable but grows GDP-plus, not 30% — it cannot carry a 55x multiple alone. Any commercial-ramp disappointment, margin-dilution surprise, or NRC delay de-rates a stock priced for near-flawless execution. Confirmation: organic growth decelerating below ~10%, commercial EBITDA margin failing to climb, FCF guidance cut on capex, or an enrichment/NRC timeline push-out.
BWXT.sop_bull: $245-255/sh. Government Operations: ~$479M FY25 adj EBITDA growing ~8%, value at 16x (defense-aerospace, sole-source naval premium) = ~$7.7B EV. Commercial Operations: ~$110M FY25 adj EBITDA growing 30-50% with SMR/HALEU/medical optionality, value at 28-32x forward as a high-growth nuclear-reshoring pure-play = ~$3.5-4.0B EV. Sum operating EV ~$11.2-11.7B... but the bull case is a multiple/optionality story: applying a 33x EV/EBITDA on ~$655M FY26 guided adj EBITDA = ~$21.6B EV, less ~$1.5B net debt = ~$20.1B equity / ~91M sh ~ $220, plus HALEU enrichment + space-microreactor + medical-isotope call-options worth ~$25-35/sh in a successful commercial-ramp scenario => ~$245-255. Backlog ($8.65B) underwrites the revenue line and de-risks the EBITDA bridge.
BWXT.sop_bear: $140-160/sh. Re-rate to a defense-quality multiple. Government Operations ~$479M EBITDA at 14x = ~$6.7B EV; Commercial ~$110M EBITDA at 16x (margin-dilutive, capex-heavy, execution-gated) = ~$1.8B EV; total operating EV ~$8.5B less ~$1.5B net debt = ~$7.0B equity. On a blended-company basis: ~$655M FY26 EBITDA at 18-20x EV/EBITDA (still a premium to defense peers for the moat) = ~$11.8-13.1B EV, less ~$1.5B net debt = ~$10.3-11.6B equity / ~91M sh = ~$113-128; a less harsh 22x = ~$143. Bear midpoint ~$150 — i.e. the AI/SMR premium fully unwinds and you hold a quality defense compounder at ~30x earnings.
BWXT.sop_breakdown: Whole-company base case: FY26 guided adj EBITDA ~$655M at a 26-28x EV/EBITDA blended multiple — a justified premium to defense peers (~13-15x) for the WIDE sole-source naval moat + genuine commercial-nuclear growth, but a discount to the current ~30-33x because ROIC is only ~10.5% and the commercial optionality is lightly de-risked. 27x x $655M = ~$17.7B EV, less ~$1.5B net debt = ~$16.2B equity / ~91M sh ~ $178; add ~$12-18/sh for HALEU/SMR/medical optionality not yet in EBITDA = ~$190-196. Cross-check on earnings: ~$4.68 FY26 EPS x 40-42x (premium-but-not-peak) = ~$187-197. Segment cross-check: Gov ~$7.2B EV (15x) + Commercial ~$2.6B EV (24x) + ~$0.4B options - $1.5B net debt = ~$8.7B... which is well below the market and the multiple view; the gap IS the priced-in optionality. Base fair ~$192, i.e. modestly below the ~$205 spot.
BWXT.risk_reward: Unfavorable-to-neutral. Spot ~$205. Upside-to-fair ~$192 base is NEGATIVE (~-6%); upside to the bull ~$250 is +22%; downside to the bear ~$150 is -27%. Risk/reward ~0.8:1 against (downside-to-break exceeds upside-to-fair-value). Even on the bull/bear span the asymmetry is roughly symmetric (+22% / -27%), and the base case sits slightly below spot — you are buying a strengthening business at a price that already discounts a successful commercial ramp.
BWXT.catalyst_status: SOFT_EXTENDED. The load-bearing commercial catalysts are real but non-binding, third-party-dependent and serially-extending: (1) Tennessee HEU/HALEU enrichment facility is at the NRC-engagement stage (multi-year licensing, no fixed in-service date) — confirmed in Q1-26 call (05/04/2026); (2) SMR/microreactor component volumes depend on third-party reactor deployments (Oklo, X-energy et al.) that keep slipping; (3) Mount Vernon greenfield is 'being evaluated,' not committed. The HARD, already-fired catalysts (Q1-26 beat-and-raise, $8.65B backlog print, PCG acquisition announced) are spent — the stock re-rated to 55x ON them. So the live catalysts are SOFT_EXTENDED (regulatory/third-party, no binding date), while the re-rating drivers are FIRED. No PENDING_HARD dated event with real asymmetry in the next 3-9 months.
BWXT.peer_comps_note: BWXT sits in scale-out Basket E (POWER-GEN) as a picks-and-shovels nuclear toll. TRUE peers split across two cohorts. (1) Nuclear-fuel/enrichment pure-plays: Centrus Energy (LEU) — Q1-26 adj EPS $1.05, $900M HALEU award, raised rev guide $450-500M, signed non-binding LOI w/ Oklo 06/18/2026 for 2029 HALEU; far smaller, lumpier, more direct HALEU leverage but no defense annuity. Cameco (CCJ) — uranium miner, 2026 EPS +17.5% / 2027 +58.7% (Zacks), upstream-fuel exposure, trades at a high uranium-cycle multiple. (2) Defense/nuclear-component primes: Curtiss-Wright (CW, naval/defense nuclear components, reported Q1-26) — closest defense-multiple anchor at ~25-28x earnings; mainline A&D primes (LMT/GD/HII) trade ~15-18x. On the comp grid BWXT ranks: VALUATION — most expensive of the defense cohort (~44x fwd P/E, ~30-33x fwd EV/EBITDA, a ~2.4x premium to defense peers); cheaper than the SMR-developer cohort (Oklo/SMR, pre-earnings, no comparable multiple) but far more profitable. GROWTH — top-quartile among profitable nuclear names (26% rev, +77% backlog) but below pure-play enrichment optionality (LEU). MARGINS — best-in-class government segment (20.4% EBITDA) vs Centrus/commercial peers; blended diluting toward commercial. RETURNS — ~10.5% ROIC is MIDDLE of the pack, below high-return defense electronics (CW ~13-15%), undermining the premium multiple. AI-CAPEX EXPOSURE — high-quality and DEFENSE-FLOORED: unlike pure-SMR plays whose entire thesis is hyperscaler power, BWXT's Navy spine keeps paying if AI-capex pauses — the most durable demand profile in the basket, which partly justifies a premium but not 2x the cohort. Net: BWXT is the highest-quality, most-de-risked name in POWER-GEN, priced accordingly — the premium is earned on moat/durability but stretched on returns/valuation.
BWXT.role_in_scaleout: BWXT is the defense-floored nuclear toll of the build-out: it supplies the reactor components, special materials, HALEU/enrichment reshoring and SMR/microreactor manufacturing that the AI-power/SMR layer must pass through — and because its government spine (sole-source U.S. naval reactors) keeps paying regardless of hyperscaler capex, its demand is the most durable and least AI-capex-dependent in the basket; the commercial-nuclear upside is real but multi-year and regulatory-gated.
BWXT.consensus_delta: There is little exploitable consensus delta here — the street is NOT looking the wrong way; it has fully embraced BWXT as 'the nuclear company' and re-rated it to ~55x trailing / ~44x forward earnings with a ~$238 average target (+19%). The expectations-arbitrage cuts AGAINST a long: consensus already extrapolates the ~30% commercial growth and treats the HALEU/SMR optionality as near-certain, when (a) organic growth is only ~11% (rest is M&A), (b) commercial's 12.9% EBITDA margin dilutes the 20.4% government margin so blended-margin expansion is structurally capped, and (c) the enrichment/SMR catalysts are NRC- and third-party-gated with no binding dates. The mispricing is on the QUALITY-OF-GROWTH and RETURNS axis: the multiple implies a returns profile (mid-teens+ ROIC, accelerating organic) that a ~10.5% ROIC, M&A-assisted grower does not deliver. The bullish street assumption most likely to be wrong: that commercial scales to government-like profitability quickly — the mix shift says the opposite near-term.
BWXT.valley_of_death: No solvency or cash-burn cliff — the Navy backlog and ~$315-330M guided FCF insulate the balance sheet; ~$1.5B net debt at ~2.3x EBITDA is investment-grade-comfortable. The 3-9 month risk is MULTIPLE, not fundamental: (1) capex/greenfield cash drag — Mount Vernon evaluation + PCG integration consume cash before generating it, and any FCF-guidance trim on capex would hit a stock priced at a ~1.6% FCF yield hard; (2) a single soft commercial quarter or an NRC/enrichment timeline push-out would puncture the 'accelerating commercial' narrative that justifies the premium; (3) basket-level AI-capex de-grossing risk — the regime doc flags AI-capex as ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the dominant shared factor; a hyperscaler capex scare would de-rate the entire POWER-GEN cohort (Oklo/SMR/LEU/BWXT) together even though BWXT's Navy spine is orthogonal. BWXT would fall LESS than the pure-plays but would not be spared a cohort de-rate from a 55x starting point.
BWXT.positioning_washout: No yield-base washout risk — the 0.36% dividend means there is no income-holder cohort to force-sell, and only ~$20M of buybacks means no support-bid either. The shareholder base is growth/thematic momentum money that bought the nuclear-renaissance re-rating; that is the vulnerability — momentum ownership at 55x unwinds violently on any growth-narrative crack or a broad AI-capex de-grossing, with no value/income floor underneath to absorb it. There is no mechanical forced-seller catalyst to exploit on the LONG side here; if anything the washout risk is asymmetric to the downside.
BWXT.forcing_function: No clean near-term forcing function that re-rates UP — the hard catalysts (Q1-26 beat-and-raise 05/04/2026; $8.65B backlog print; PCG announcement) already FIRED and the re-rating is spent. The next scheduled events are Q2-26 earnings (~early Aug 2026, another beat-and-raise is largely expected/priced) and incremental, undated NRC engagement on the Tennessee enrichment facility plus a Mount Vernon go/no-go decision — none binding, none dated. The realistic forcing function is to the DOWNSIDE: a soft commercial quarter, a capex-driven FCF trim, an NRC/SMR timeline slip, or a cohort-wide AI-capex de-grossing that resets the 55x multiple.
BWXT.moderator_conclusion: BWXT is the highest-quality, most-durable name in scale-out Basket E — a genuine WIDE-moat sole-source naval-nuclear monopoly with a real, strengthening commercial-nuclear ramp and best-in-class $8.65B backlog (+77% YoY). The INTERROGATOR is right: trajectory STRENGTHENING, moat WIDE/STABLE, no secular threat — it is a beneficiary, not a victim, of the AI-power/SMR/reshoring waves. But the CRO mandate is timing, saturation and margin-of-safety, and on all three the name fails as a fresh long. APPLYING THE REGIME (2026-06-05 read): the doc rewards HARD-DATED catalysts inside the favorable window and PENALIZES already-fired/already-ran catalysts, rate-rescue theses, and names whose thesis hinges on the SAME shared macro factor (AI-capex durability) as the rest of the basket. BWXT's re-rating catalysts are FIRED (the beat-and-raise and backlog print already drove it to 55x); the LIVE catalysts (HALEU/NRC, SMR, Mount Vernon) are SOFT_EXTENDED — non-binding, third-party/regulatory-gated, no dates; and the thesis shares the basket's single dominant resolution driver (AI-capex), so it FAILS the §1.7 book-independence test — it would de-rate alongside Oklo/SMR/LEU in any AI-capex scare. Base-case SoP fair value ~$192 sits modestly BELOW the ~$205 spot; risk/reward is ~0.8:1 against (downside-to-bear ~-27% vs upside-to-fair ~-6%, upside-to-bull +22%). The ~2.4x premium to defense peers is earned on durability but stretched against a ~10.5% ROIC and ~1.6% FCF yield. VERDICT C / conviction 2: the business is excellent but the stock is saturated, the re-rating is spent, and there is no asymmetric hard catalyst in-window — this is a 'watch for a flush' name, not an entry. The specific event to wait for: a broad AI-capex de-grossing or a single soft commercial quarter that resets the multiple toward the high-30s P/E, where the WIDE moat + secular tailwind would make it a high-conviction durable compounder. VALUE_CONVICTION 3: stripped of catalyst/regime, the value case is a quality WIDE-moat franchise trading modestly above a defensible intrinsic — not cheap (no margin of safety at spot), not a trap (durable, growing, no secular erosion) — a fair price for a great business, capped at 3 by the absence of any discount to fair value.
BWXT.interrogator_dossier:
# BWX Technologies (BWXT) — Forensic Dossier

*Interrogator pass, Claude Opus 4.8. Scale-out Basket E (POWER-GEN). Data: Q1-2026 release + call (05/04/2026), FY2025 results (02/23/2026), 10-Q (3/31/2026), live quote 06/21/2026. No data bundled — all web-sourced.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

BWXT's story has moved through three distinct phases. For most of the last decade it was framed as a **defense monopoly with an annuity** — the sole manufacturer of naval nuclear reactors for the U.S. Navy's Virginia/Columbia submarines and Ford carriers, a regulated-margin, low-beta, GDP-plus compounder. Over 2024–2025 management widened the lens to **"the nuclear company"**, deliberately attaching the franchise to three secular waves: (1) the AI-datacenter power crunch and the SMR/microreactor build-out, (2) HALEU/enrichment domestic-supply reshoring, and (3) medical isotopes. By Q1-2026 the narrative has tilted decisively **commercial-first**: management now leads with commercial nuclear, raised commercial growth guidance to ~30% (from "high-teens"), and is funding a U.S. commercial-nuclear manufacturing footprint via the Precision Components Group (PCG) acquisition plus a contemplated greenfield at Mount Vernon, Indiana. The government franchise is now positioned as the durable cash spine that funds the commercial option. Growth drivers ADDED across the arc: SMR components, HEU/HALEU enrichment (Tennessee NRC engagement), space microreactors, localized "Made-in-USA" capacity. Nothing material has been quietly dropped — medical isotopes (Tc-99m / actinium) remain a slow-burn call-option.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Mgmt claim | The number | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| "Record demand, accelerating commercial" | Commercial rev +63% FY25 to $853M; +121% in Q1-26; total rev +18% FY25, +26% Q1-26 (11% organic) | **Corroborated.** Acceleration is real and partly M&A-fueled (Kinectrics, A.O.T., now PCG) but organic 11% is genuine. |
| "Record backlog, multi-year visibility" | Backlog $7.3B end-FY25 (+50%) → **$8.65–8.7B Q1-26 (+77% YoY, +19% QoQ)** | **Corroborated, and the single strongest fact.** Backlog ~2.3x annual revenue. |
| "Profitable, expanding" | Op margin ~12.7% FY25; Gov EBITDA margin 20.4%, Commercial 12.9%; adj EBITDA +14% Q1-26 | **Mixed.** Government margin is rich and stable; commercial dilutes the blended margin as mix shifts. Adjusted EBITDA grows but slower than revenue — margin is being traded for growth. |
| "Strong cash generation" | FCF $295M FY25; 2026 guide raised to $315–330M | **Corroborated**, though FCF/share is modest vs an $18.7B cap (~1.6% FCF yield). |
| "Disciplined balance sheet" | ~$2.0B debt, ~$0.51B cash → **~$1.5B net debt**; leverage ~2.3x EBITDA | **Adequate, not pristine.** Leverage rising as it funds M&A + greenfield capex. Investment-grade-like, covered by Navy cash, but no longer a net-cash story. |
| "Returns justify the franchise" | ROIC ~10.5% | **The soft spot.** A ~10.5% ROIC against a 55x P/E is the central tension — quality, not exceptional capital efficiency. The moat is contractual/regulatory, not a returns-on-capital fortress. |

Where the story is **richer than the numbers**: the valuation embeds commercial-nuclear/SMR optionality that is still a single-digit-% of EBITDA. Where it is **poorer**: the government backlog and Navy sole-source position are arguably under-celebrated relative to how durable they are.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Rising.** The cadence of guidance raises (FY26 revenue lifted to >$3.75B, EBITDA to $650–665M, EPS to $4.60–4.75, FCF to $315–330M after only Q1) signals offensive posture. Language is investment/growth-oriented ("customers increasingly asking for localized U.S. capacity," "evaluating a larger greenfield"), not defensive cost-language. Management is leaning into capex and M&A rather than guarding margin — a confidence tell. The only defensive note is the careful framing of enrichment/NRC timelines as multi-year regulatory processes.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**High.** Q1-26 EPS $1.12 beat by ~$0.20 (+22% YoY); FY25 was a record beat-and-raise; the company raised full-year guidance one quarter in. Pattern is **consistent modest sandbagging then raise** — a reliable, conservatively-guided cadence typical of a cost-plus defense base layered with commercial upside. Reliability: **High.**

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

- **Valuation/multiple** — the recurring pushback is not on the business but on 55x trailing / ~44x forward earnings and ~30–33x EV/EBITDA (a ~2.4x premium to defense peers). Management answers with backlog and TAM, not with a returns bridge.
- **Commercial margin dilution** — as commercial (12.9% EBITDA margin) grows faster than government (20.4%), blended margins compress; analysts probe whether commercial scales to government-like profitability.
- **Capex/greenfield cash drag** — Mount Vernon and PCG integration are cash uses before they are cash generators; the FCF-yield gap vs the multiple is the unspoken worry.
- **Enrichment timeline reality** — HEU/HALEU Tennessee facility is NRC-gated and years out; risk of it being priced as near-term.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🟢 Backlog $8.65–8.7B, +77% YoY — best-in-class visibility (Q1-26).
🟢 Sole-source Navy nuclear position — regulatory/security-clearance moat, effectively un-disruptable (structural).
🟢 Beat-and-raise cadence intact; guidance lifted after Q1 (Q1-26).
🟢 Genuine secular tailwind: AI-power, SMR, HALEU reshoring all converge on BWXT's competencies.
🔴 ~55x trailing P/E on a ~10.5% ROIC, ~1.6% FCF yield — the AI/SMR optionality is heavily pre-paid by the market.
🔴 Net debt ~$1.5B and rising; M&A + greenfield capex turn a former net-cash compounder leverage-up.
🔴 Commercial growth is partly acquired (Kinectrics/A.O.T./PCG) — organic is a healthier ~11%, so headline 26% overstates underlying momentum.
🔴 Commercial-mix margin dilution structurally caps blended-margin expansion near-term.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

The terminology shift from "naval nuclear / defense" toward "**the nuclear company**" and "localized U.S. capacity" is a deliberate repositioning to capture the AI-power and reshoring narrative multiple — and it has worked (the stock re-rated to 55x). Capital allocation has quietly pivoted from buybacks/dividend (only ~$20M repurchased in 2025, 0.36% yield) toward **growth M&A and greenfield capex** — management is signaling it sees higher-return reinvestment than returning cash, consistent with the offensive tone but raising the execution bar. The repeated "customers asking for U.S. capacity" framing is positioning BWXT as the reshoring beneficiary of a fragmented commercial-nuclear supply chain.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Trajectory: STRENGTHENING.** Backlog, bookings, guidance and tone all point up, with a credible multi-secular demand stack and a genuine sole-source defense spine. The single most important thing the director must weigh before sizing: **how much of the commercial-nuclear / SMR / HALEU optionality is already in the 55x multiple** — the *business* is strengthening, but the *stock* has priced in a large chunk of a still-nascent commercial ramp; you are paying a wide-moat multiple for a ~10.5%-ROIC compounder whose terminal upside is real but lightly de-risked.

**Moat:** regulatory-license + scale-cost + security-clearance intangible. On the **government** side it is **WIDE** (sole U.S. naval-nuclear-reactor source, decades of qualification, classified-facility barriers) and **STABLE** — effectively un-disruptable, the opposite of a secular-decline name. On the **commercial** side the moat is narrower and being built, not defended. Blended: **WIDE but not widening on returns** — ROIC is ~10.5% and roughly flat-to-down as commercial dilutes, so I score MOAT_TREND **STABLE** (not widening — the franchise is durable but capital efficiency is not improving).

**Secular threat: NONE / manageable.** BWXT is a *beneficiary* of the energy-transition + AI-power + reshoring secular waves, not a victim. There is no AI-displacement, disintermediation, or demand-peak risk to its terminal value; the only "secular" risk is the inverse — that the commercial-nuclear renaissance under-delivers vs the priced-in expectation (a valuation risk, not a terminal-erosion risk). Cheapness is irrelevant here — this is the opposite case: a quality franchise at a rich price.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: WIDE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none

skeptic.corrections: The debate frames the Tennessee 'HEU/HALEU enrichment' facility as part of the commercial AI-scale-out optionality. Primary sources show it is materially WEAKER and DIFFERENT: the Erwin, TN facility is HEU for the U.S. Navy under a $1.5B NNSA/DOE contract (Sept 2025) — i.e. DEFENSE supply, not commercial HALEU for SMRs/AI-power; BWXT only NOTIFIED the NRC of intent (pre-application) in April 2026, the actual license application is not expected until Q1-2027, and the facility is targeted to be operational only ~2035. So this is not a 3-9 month catalyst and is not AI-scale-out demand at all; it should be removed from the priced-in commercial call-option. This corrects the catalyst toward SOFTER/LATER than 'SOFT_EXTENDED' and slightly reduces the bull's optionality stack. All other load-bearing numbers verify exactly: backlog $8.65B (Gov $6.93B + Commercial $1.72B), bookings $2.25B, Q1-26 rev $860.2M, commercial rev $283.6M (+121%), net debt $1,505.5M ($2,017.9M debt − $512.4M cash), ~91.9M diluted shares, FY26 guide raised (rev >$3.75B, adj EBITDA $650-665M, EPS $4.60-4.75, FCF $315-330M). Spot ~$205-207, ~$19B cap, ~43x fwd P/E, avg target ~$238 (+17%) — confirms a name that has run hard with consensus already bullish.

CARR.sector: Industrials — HVAC / Climate & Energy Solutions (data-center thermal management)
CARR.bull_thesis: Carrier is a re-simplified, investment-grade (BBB+) HVAC pure-play that has bolted a genuine AI-cooling growth vector onto a durable aftermarket annuity. The data-center thesis is real and inflecting: Q1'26 global DC orders up over 500% YoY, CHVAC orders up 35%, the QuantumLeap integrated DC solution (next-gen chillers +30% efficiency, direct-to-chip CDUs at 1/3/5MW, Nlyte DCIM over 300 DCs and 1M+ racks) winning hundreds of millions in orders, and the ZutaCore two-phase liquid-cooling partnership positioning Carrier across both air and direct-to-chip thermal. DC revenue grew ~$1B (2025) toward a $1.5B 2026 target that backlog 'fully covers' with management 'targeting to exceed.' Crucially, Carrier is wiring a recurring-service annuity (Lynx ~240k subs, connected devices +25%, 6th straight year of double-digit aftermarket growth targeted, internal 13-14% target) on top of the equipment win — converting cyclical thermal sales into a high-margin, asset-light annuity, the same playbook that took CHVAC to +80% sales / +130% backlog / margins tripled / +500bps share since spin. The portfolio is cleaner post-Viessmann-in / Fire & Security + Refrigeration-out, earning an S&P upgrade to BBB+. Pricing power held (+2pts 2026) despite pushback. Buyback shrank the share count 5.5%, supporting per-share value. If DC + CHVAC + aftermarket grow fast enough to swing the consolidated line positive in 12-24 months while resi/China stabilize, the flat-base optics give way to a mid-single-digit-plus organic compounder with a structural AI-capex and electrification tailwind — and the multiple re-rates toward the Trane (~30x fwd) / JCI (~25x fwd) cohort. Conditions: DC orders must convert to revenue, China must stop bleeding, and aftermarket must keep compounding double-digit.
CARR.bear_thesis: At the consolidated level Carrier is currently a flat-to-shrinking, margin-compressing, sub-WACC business dressed in an AI-cooling narrative. FY2025 revenue fell 3.3% (TTM -1.9%); Q1'26 adjusted EPS fell 12% YoY; consolidated operating margin compressed from ~11.8% (FY24) to ~10.0% (FY25); and ROIC of ~4.6% sits well BELOW WACC of ~8.6% — the company is destroying economic value right now, a hangover from $13B of Viessmann goodwill/debt (~$10.5B net debt) against depressed earnings. The headline AI-cooling vector is only ~2-2.5% of a $22B base: even hitting $1.5B DC in 2026 adds ~$500M while residential (down 12%, distributor movements -8% on 6%+ mortgages), China RLC (-25%, 'hard to call a bottom'), and a 'disappointing' Europe segment margin drag the whole. Management reaffirmed — did NOT raise — flat-to-LSD organic and ~$2.80 EPS, i.e. they are promising stability, not growth. The +2pts of 2026 pricing is 75% tariff-linked and explicitly a dollar-for-dollar pass-through, not margin-accretive, and rolls off if tariffs reverse. Management's vocabulary has rotated from the under-delivered heat-pump/Viessmann story to data-center wherever the capex is, and flatters the deck with cumulative-since-spin stats because the current-year organic line is flat. Most damning for entry: at ~24x forward P/E the stock has already discovered the AI-cooling story and trades above its own ~16-21x history on a flat-growth, eroding-returns base. You are paying a growth multiple for a company guiding to no growth, with the optionality already priced. What confirms the bear: DC orders that build backlog but don't convert, China that stays a bottomless drag, and EPS that keeps the buyback doing the heavy lifting.
CARR.sop_bull: ~$80-85/share. Whole-company intrinsic on 2027E earnings power, crediting the DC ramp. Base 2026E adj EPS ~$2.80 growing to ~$3.10-3.20 in 2027 as DC scales past $1.5B, CHVAC compounds and resi/China normalize. Apply 26x (a slight discount to JCI ~25x and Trane ~30x, premium to Lennox ~21x, justified by the AI-cooling optionality and aftermarket annuity) to ~$3.15 => ~$82. Cross-check on EV/EBITDA: ~$3.0B EBITDA x 18-19x = ~$55-57B EV, less ~$10.5B net debt = ~$45-47B equity / ~840M shares = ~$53-56... the EPS-multiple path is the bull frame and dominates given the growth credit. Embedded free option on the data-center recurring-service annuity (Nlyte + Lynx) worth several $/share if it scales like the building aftermarket. Bull SoP: ~$82, supported by buyback shrinking the count.
CARR.sop_bear: ~$52-58/share. The flat-base, sub-WACC reality. Capitalize current power: 2026E adj EPS ~$2.80 at a peer-low-but-still-fair ~20x (Lennox-like, reflecting flat growth + eroding returns) = ~$56. EV/EBITDA cross-check: ~$2.9-3.0B EBITDA x 16x = ~$47B EV, less ~$10.5B net debt = ~$36-37B equity / ~840M shares = ~$43-44 on a conservative multiple; blending the EPS path up for the clean balance sheet and aftermarket quality lands ~$52-55. Bear SoP: ~$54 — i.e. the stock is already at-or-above adverse fair value, with downside to the low-$50s if DC orders disappoint or China deepens.
CARR.sop_breakdown: Base-case whole-company intrinsic (segments don't warrant separate listed multiples — Carrier is now a focused HVAC platform, not a conglomerate). Anchor: 2026E adj EPS ~$2.80, with modest credit for DC/CHVAC/aftermarket lifting 2027 toward ~$3.00. Fair multiple ~22x (premium to Lennox ~21x for the AI-cooling + aftermarket optionality, discount to JCI ~25x / Trane ~30x for the sub-WACC ROIC, flat consolidated revenue and margin compression) => ~$64. EV/EBITDA cross-check: ~$3.0B EBITDA x ~17.5x = ~$52.5B EV, less ~$10.5B net debt = ~$42B equity / ~840M sh = ~$50; the EPS-multiple path is more representative for an EPS-guided industrial and is weighted higher. Reconciled SoP fair value ~$62-66. Embedded but unpriced: the DC recurring-service annuity call option (Nlyte/Lynx) — upside lever, not in the base.
CARR.risk_reward: Poor-to-balanced at ~$70. Upside-to-fair (~$64 mid) is roughly FLAT-to-negative (stock trades slightly ABOVE base-case SoP); bull case ~$82 = ~+17%. Downside-to-bear (~$54) = ~-23%. So at the current price the asymmetry is unfavorable: ~17% upside vs ~23% downside, ~0.7:1 — you are paying up for optionality that is largely priced. A pullback to the high-$50s/low-$60s would flip the ratio to attractive (~+30%/-10%).
CARR.peer_comps_note: True peers are the pure/near-pure HVAC platforms, ranked on AI-capex (data-center thermal) exposure: Trane Technologies (TT) and Johnson Controls (JCI) are the highest-AI-exposed (TT's applied-systems + DC chillers; JCI's data-center/Silent-Aire buildout), Lennox (LII) is the most resi/light-commercial and lowest DC exposure, with Daikin/Mitsubishi Electric as global comps. LIVE multiples verified Jun 2026: Trane ~30.7x fwd P/E (~24.6x EV/EBITDA) — the cohort's premium AI-cooling leader; JCI ~25x fwd P/E (~27.7x EV/EBITDA); Lennox ~21.4x fwd P/E (~17.2x EV/EBITDA); Carrier ~23.6x fwd P/E (~18.1x EV/EBITDA, EV/rev ~3.1x). VALUATION rank: CARR sits mid-pack — cheaper than TT/JCI, dearer than Lennox, but above its OWN ~16-21x 2024-25 history. GROWTH: CARR is the laggard on delivered organic (-3.3% FY25, flat 2026 guide) vs TT's ~7-10% revenue growth and double-digit DC tailwind; CARR leads only on ORDER growth (DC +500%, CHVAC +35%). MARGINS: CARR's ~10% consolidated op margin trails Trane's ~18% materially — the widest peer gap. RETURNS: CARR ROIC ~4.6% < WACC ~8.6% is the worst of the cohort (Viessmann-goodwill drag); Trane earns well above WACC. AI-CAPEX EXPOSURE: CARR has a credible, fast-growing-but-small (~2-2.5% of rev) DC thermal franchise (QuantumLeap/ZutaCore/CDU); Trane's DC exposure is larger and more proven. Net: CARR is the value-relative name in the cohort on forward P/E, but for good reason — lowest margins, lowest (sub-WACC) returns, slowest delivered growth; the discount to TT/JCI is earned, not a mispricing.
CARR.role_in_scaleout: Carrier is a Tier-2 thermal supplier to the AI build-out: data-center cooling via next-gen chillers (QuantumLeap, +30% efficiency), direct-to-chip CDUs (1/3/5MW), two-phase liquid cooling (ZutaCore), and DCIM software (Nlyte) — a real but non-dominant position behind Vertiv/Trane in the cooling layer; demand is durable (every AI rack needs heat rejection and the thermal envelope is the binding physical constraint) but Carrier's share of that wallet is modest and the franchise is only ~2-2.5% of company revenue today.
CARR.consensus_delta: The street has largely CAUGHT UP to the AI-cooling story here — this is not a name where Wall Street is looking the wrong way; if anything it is looking too eagerly. 26 analysts average Buy with a ~$76 target, and the ~24x forward P/E (vs CARR's own ~16-21x 2024-25 range) embeds a re-rating that the +500% DC order print already triggered. The genuinely under-appreciated leg is NOT the DC equipment win (priced) but the DURABILITY question the bulls gloss: consensus models the DC ramp and CHVAC share gains but under-weights that (a) ROIC is ~4.6% vs ~8.6% WACC — the consolidated business destroys value today, (b) the +2pts 2026 pricing is a dollar-for-dollar tariff pass-through, not margin-accretive, and (c) management REAFFIRMED rather than RAISED flat-to-LSD organic despite a clean Q1 beat. The contrarian read is bearish-to-neutral, not bullish: the optimistic assumption that is factually soft is 'DC + CHVAC swing the whole-company line to growth soon' — orders are +500% but consolidated revenue is still -1.9% TTM, and DC is only ~2-2.5% of the base. The market is paying a growth multiple for a no-growth 2026 guide.
CARR.valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9mo) risks that hit before any AI-cooling payoff converts: (1) Residential — 30-yr mortgage above 6%, distributor-to-dealer unit movements -8%, plus the R-454B refrigerant-transition pre-buy hangover; resi was -12% in Q1 and the spring/summer cooling season carries the year. (2) China — RLC -25%, management 'hard to call a bottom'; a property-driven drag with no visible inflection that could deepen. (3) Europe margin 'disappointing' on promotions + soft commercial volume; heat-pump subsidy timing is lumpy. (4) Tariff/pricing reversal risk — 75% of the +2pts pricing is Section 232-linked; a tariff rollback removes both the price and (since it is pass-through) creates an awkward optics air-pocket. (5) Order-to-revenue lag — the bull case rests on backlog conversion; any slip leaves the stock owning a 24x multiple on a flat, sub-WACC P&L. No debt-maturity wall crisis (IG, BBB+, ~$10.5B net debt is serviceable) and FCF is positive (~$1.7B TTM), so this is an earnings/optics valley, not a solvency one.
CARR.positioning_washout: No forced-seller dynamic. Carrier is an S&P 500 / large-cap industrial held broadly by index, quality, and dividend-growth funds; the dividend is modest and growing (no cut risk to trigger yield-holder liquidation), and the buyback is a steady bid (shares -5.5% YoY). There is no pivot, dividend cut, or index event forcing mechanical selling. The flip side: because there is no washout setup, there is also no capitulation entry to wait for via positioning — the only thing that resets the entry is a fundamental disappointment (resi/China/DC-conversion miss) de-rating the multiple. This is a 'wait for the multiple to come to you' name, not a 'wait for the forced seller' name.
CARR.forcing_function: The load-bearing dates are quarterly earnings — Q2'26 (late Jul/early Aug 2026, guided to just under $6B revenue and ~$0.80 adj EPS) and Q3'26 — where the market will judge whether DC orders are CONVERTING to revenue and whether China/resi have bottomed. There is no discrete corporate event (no spin, proxy, or deal) to force a re-rating; the re-rate already happened on the Q1'26 +500% DC order print. The next forcing function cuts both ways and is more likely a DE-rating catalyst than a further re-rate: a quarter where flat consolidated revenue persists despite the order book would puncture the growth multiple. A genuine raise of the $1.5B DC target (management is 'targeting to exceed') on a future call is the bull's forcing function, but it is incremental, not transformational.
CARR.moderator_conclusion: VERDICT C (PASS), conviction 2. Applying the current regime (CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME §3-4: complacent, fully-invested melt-up with AI-capex as the single dominant load-bearing factor under the tape; §1.7 demands resolution-driver independence): CARR fails the independence test hardest — its entire upside thesis hinges on the SAME shared macro driver (AI-capex durability) as the rest of scale-out Basket C and much of the book, with no idiosyncratic catalyst to diversify the resolution path. The regime explicitly PENALIZES already-fired / already-ran catalysts, and CARR's load-bearing catalyst — the +500% DC order inflection and QuantumLeap launch — is FIRED: it printed in Q1'26, the stock re-rated from ~16-21x to ~24x forward on it, and analysts are now Buy-consensus. There is no hard-dated, in-window, independent catalyst to reward; the next forcing function (Q2/Q3 prints) is more likely a de-rating test of order-to-revenue conversion. On valuation the name is at-or-slightly-above its reconciled ~$62-66 SoP fair value at ~$70, giving unfavorable ~0.7:1 risk/reward (~+17% bull vs ~-23% bear). The peer comps confirm the discount to TT/JCI is EARNED (lowest margins ~10% vs Trane ~18%, sub-WACC ROIC ~4.6%, slowest delivered growth), not a mispricing. How much AI optionality is priced: most of it — the DC franchise is only ~2-2.5% of revenue yet the multiple already reflects the narrative. This is a good, cleaner-balance-sheet, investment-grade HVAC franchise with a real (if small) AI-cooling call option and a durable aftermarket annuity — but at this price, in this regime, with a fired-and-priced catalyst and no resolution-driver independence, it is a PASS for the catalyst book. value_conviction 3 (NOT higher): stripped of any catalyst/AI overlay, the pure value case is capped — the moat is NARROW and returns are ERODING (ROIC ~4.6% < WACC ~8.6%, margins compressing), and the moat-aware rule caps an eroding-returns name at 3; the franchise quality and clean balance sheet keep it from lower, but a flat-growth, sub-WACC business at ~24x forward is fairly-to-fully valued, not cheap. WATCHLIST trigger: a de-rate to the high-$50s/low-$60s (toward Lennox-like ~21x) would flip risk/reward attractive and warrant a fresh look as a quality-compounder-at-a-fair-price — the AI-cooling annuity is the kind of free option worth owning, just not at this entry.
CARR.interrogator_dossier:
# CARRIER GLOBAL (CARR) — Forensic Dossier

*Interrogator pass, Opus 4.8. Universe role: scale-out Basket C (THERMAL) — data-center cooling / HVAC at scale. Built from the Q1 2026 earnings call (Apr 30, 2026), the Q4/FY2025 release, and the live financial trajectory. Price ~$70, market cap ~$58B (Jun 2026).*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

Carrier's story has gone through three acts since the 2020 UTC spin. Act one (2020-2022): a stand-alone HVAC pure-play deleveraging and proving margins. Act two (2023-2024): the **transformation** — the $13B Viessmann Climate Solutions acquisition to bolt on European heat pumps, paired with the sale of Fire & Security, Commercial Refrigeration, and the Industrial Fire business. Management reframed CARR as 'the global leader in intelligent climate and energy solutions,' pivoting the narrative from cyclical box-mover to secular-energy-transition platform (heat pumps, electrification, aftermarket/connected).

Act three (2025-2026, the current chapter): the **data-center/AI-cooling vector**. By Q1 2026 the load-bearing growth story is explicitly thermal management of AI compute. Management now leads with: global data-center orders **up over 500%** YoY, CHVAC orders **up 35%**, the **QuantumLeap** integrated DC solution (launched Feb 2025) winning 'hundreds of millions' in orders, CDU portfolio expansion (1/3/5MW), and the **ZutaCore** two-phase liquid-cooling partnership. The energy-transition (heat-pump) story has quietly faded into the background as European boiler demand softened and subsidy timing wobbled; it is now a steady-state contributor, not the headline. The aftermarket/connected-services flywheel (Lynx subscriptions ~240k, >25% connected-device growth, targeting a 6th consecutive year of double-digit aftermarket growth) is the durable through-line across all three acts.

Net: the arc has **expanded then re-centered** — from 'HVAC pure-play' to 'climate platform' to 'AI-cooling + aftermarket compounder.' The data-center pivot is genuine (orders are real and large in % terms) but is being grafted onto a core that is, at the consolidated level, **flat-to-shrinking**.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Management claim (Q1'26 / FY25) | The hard numbers | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| 'Strong order momentum, company orders +11%' | Reported Q1 revenue $5.3B, **organic sales ~flat**; FY2025 revenue **$21.75B, -3.3% YoY**; TTM -1.9% | Orders != revenue. The order book is genuinely inflecting (DC +500%, CHVAC +35%) but the **delivered top line is contracting**. |
| '$1.5B data-center sales target for 2026, targeting to exceed' | DC was ~$1B in 2025 -> $1.5B 2026E = ~$500M incremental on a **$22B** base (~2.3% of revenue) | Real and growing fast, but **small relative to the whole**. |
| 'CHVAC since spin: sales +80%, backlog +130%, margins tripled, +500bps share' | Consolidated operating margin **compressed**: FY24 ~11.8% -> FY25 ~10.0%; Q1'26 adj EPS $0.57 **-12% YoY** | Segment-true, company-false — diluted by resi/China. |
| 'Aftermarket: 6th straight year of double-digit growth' | Connected devices +25% Q1; Lynx ~240k subs | **Corroborated** — the real moat-builder. |
| '~$22B revenue, ~$2.80 adj EPS reaffirmed' | Flat-to-LSD organic + $250M Riello exit headwind | Guidance is **defensive/flat**. |
| 'Returned ~$500M; buybacks reducing share count' | Shares **-5.5% YoY** | **Corroborated.** |
| FCF strength | Q1 FCF -$15M (seasonal); TTM FCF **$1.69B**, margin ~7.7% | Solid but unspectacular. |

**Core divergence:** the narrative is a growth/AI-cooling story; the consolidated financials are a flat-revenue, margin-compressing, EPS-declining (-12% Q1) story rescued at the per-share line by buybacks.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Rating: Stable, tilting cautiously-offensive on data center / defensive on the consumer core.** Offensive on DC ('targeting to exceed' $1.5B) and aftermarket ('we have the aftermarket playbook'); confident on pricing (+2pts, held despite pushback). Defensive/candid on residential ('30-year mortgage above 6%'), China ('hard to call a bottom,' RLC -25%) and Europe margin ('disappointing'). The dual register is honest rather than evasive — Medium-High credibility.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Score: Medium-High.** Q1'26 a clean beat (EPS $0.57 vs $0.51, +11.8%; revenue $5.34B vs ~$5.0B, +6.8%) with full-year guide REAFFIRMED not raised — sandbag-lite, beat-and-hold. The 2025 portfolio transformation executed on schedule, earning an S&P upgrade to BBB+ (Mar 2025). Reliability on the plan is high; on organic growth the question is open — but they have guided flat, so little to miss.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

Order-to-revenue conversion lag (the recurring tension); China ('calling a bottom' — a persistent non-answer); residential cyclicality + R-454B refrigerant hangover; the margin bridge (consolidated compressed while CHVAC 'tripled'); pricing durability (+2pts is 75% tariff-linked, conceded dollar-for-dollar pass-through).

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

RED: consolidated revenue -3.3% FY25 / -1.9% TTM; adj EPS -12% YoY Q1'26; op margin ~11.8%->~10.0%; **ROIC ~4.6% < WACC ~8.6%** (earning below cost of capital); China RLC -25%; Europe margin disappointing; net debt ~$10.5B; AI-cooling vector only ~2-2.5% of revenue.

GREEN: DC orders +500%, CHVAC orders +35%, QuantumLeap 'hundreds of millions,' $1.5B DC target with backlog coverage; aftermarket 6th straight double-digit year, connected +25%, Lynx ~240k; portfolio simplified, S&P BBB+; buyback -5.5% share count; pricing power held; CHVAC +500bps share / margins tripled; clean beat-and-reaffirm; honest dual-register tone.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

Vocabulary rotated from 'heat pump/Viessmann' to 'data center/QuantumLeap/liquid cooling' — selling wherever the capex is, a tell the heat-pump thesis under-delivered. CHVAC 'since-spin' framing flatters because the current-year organic line is flat. DC is being positioned to seed a recurring-service annuity (QuantumLeap + Nlyte DCIM, 300+ DCs / 1M+ racks) mirroring the building-aftermarket playbook — the key unpriced optionality. 'Targeting to exceed' $1.5B is soft-guidance creep (verbal raise without a numbers raise).

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Trajectory: STABLE** (with STRENGTHENING optionality in DC/CHVAC/aftermarket on a flat-to-soft consolidated base). Not deteriorating (balance sheet improved, portfolio simplified, order book inflecting, capital return disciplined); not a growth-compounder yet (revenue shrinking, EPS -12%, ROIC < WACC). The director must weigh: the AI-cooling optionality is only ~2-2.5% of revenue today — the thesis needs DC+CHVAC to swing the whole-company line positive in 12-24mo, OR to buy the core franchise at a fair price with DC as a free call. At ~24x forward (above the ~16-21x of a year ago, rich vs a flat-growth sub-WACC base), much of that optionality is already priced.

**Moat: NARROW, ERODING.** Type = intangible-brand + installed-base/switching-cost (aftermarket) + scale-cost, with a regulatory-tailwind overlay. The aftermarket/connected annuity and CHVAC share gains are genuine wideners, BUT consolidated returns cut the other way — ROIC ~4.6% < WACC ~8.6%, margins compressed, revenue -3.3%. A high-quality segment franchise wrapped in a company earning below its cost of capital is NARROW at the consolidated level, with an ERODING returns trend.

**Secular threat: manageable (arguably a tailwind).** Carrier is on the right side of electrification/AI-capex, not a secular-decline victim. The risk is cyclical (housing/rates, China, tariff/refrigerant timing), not terminal. The valuation question is about cycle + how much AI optionality is priced, not terminal impairment.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: ERODING | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable

skeptic.kill_fact: No kill_fact breaks the (bearish) PASS thesis — every load-bearing number verifies against primary sources and the verification REINFORCES the bear: FY2025 net sales $21.75B (-3% YoY) with GAAP consolidated operating margin 10.0%, FY2025 adjusted EPS only $2.59, and Q1'26 adjusted EPS $0.57 DOWN 12% YoY (GAAP $0.28, organic sales -1%) on net debt of $10.787B. The +500% data-center order print is real but DC is a $1B->$1.5B line (~2.3%) on a $22B base; the consolidated P&L is flat-to-shrinking, margin-compressing and sub-WACC, and the ~24x forward multiple already re-rated on the fired/priced AI-cooling catalyst. The bear kill (no-growth guide at a growth multiple, no resolution-driver independence) survives intact — this is a legitimate verdict-C PASS.
skeptic.corrections: Minor figure precision only: (1) net debt is $10.278B at FY2025 year-end and $10.787B at 3/31/2026 per the 8-Ks, not '~$10.5B' — the debate's rounding straddles the two but should cite the dated value. (2) The debate implies a 2026E base ~$2.80 off a higher prior; actual FY2025 adjusted EPS was $2.59, so the '~$2.80 guide' is ~+8% YoY adjusted-EPS growth (buyback-aided), worth stating explicitly. (3) The $1.5B DC target / 'targeting to exceed' language is sourced to the Q4-2025 release & call, NOT the Q1'26 8-K (which only says backlog 'fully covers expected 2026 data center sales') — attribute it correctly. None of these alter the verdict or risk/reward.
evidence: Carrier Global FY2025 8-K / IR release (filed Feb 2026): FY2025 net sales $21.75B (-3% YoY, organic -1%), adjusted EPS $2.59, GAAP operating margin 10.0% (adjusted 15.1%), net debt $10.278B (total debt $11.833B); 2026 outlook ~$22B sales, ~$2.80 adj EPS, flat-to-LSD organic, ~$2B FCF; $1.5B 2026 DC sales target with ~$1B backlog, 'targeting to exceed' — ir.carrier.com, 2026-02

Carrier Global Q1 2026 8-K (SEC EDGAR a99-q12026earningsexhibit, filed Apr 2026): revenue $5.34B (+2% reported, -1% organic), adjusted EPS $0.57 (-12% YoY), GAAP EPS $0.28, total orders +11%, Commercial HVAC orders +35% incl. data-center orders +500%, FCF -$15M, net debt $10.787B at 3/31/2026, FY2026 ~$22B / ~$2.80 adj EPS reaffirmed (not raised) — sec.gov, 2026-04
